titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale Divinity School Library Records
titlestmt.author: Joan R. Duffy and Martha Lund Smalley

editionstmt.p: 2015 finding aid compiled by Joan R. Duffy was revised and added to by Martha Lund Smalley in 2024.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 18, 2024 at 22:25

did.abstract: These are the official archives of the Yale Divinity Library. The Divinity Library was established as a separate institution in 1932, merging the Trowbridge Reference Library, Sneath Library of Religious Education, and Day Missions Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Yale Divinity Library traces its origins to the construction of the Sterling Divinity Quadrangle in 1932. At that time, three separate collections* were moved from the original Divinity Quadrangle at the corner of College and Elm Streets to 409 Prospect Street. Rooms to house the Trowbridge Reference Library* and the Day Missions Library* were constructed within the 1932 library, and those spaces have been preserved through two major renovations (1982 and 2003). The Sneath Library of Religious Education* was merged into the general Divinity Library collection. Since 1974, the library has developed and sustained an active Special Collections department. Following the library's 2003 adaptive re-use renovation, the former Paul A. Vieth Resource Center materials and staff were assimilated into the Divinity Library as the Ministry Resource Center. A large portion of Divinity's printed and archival material now resides at the Yale Library Shelving Facility under environmentally secure conditions, and may be requested by readers for use at the Divinity Library.

For more information see: "The Yale University Divinity Library", by Raymond P. Morris. (Yale University Library Gazette, v. 43, no. 1, July 1968, pp. 33-38);"A Library Worthy of the School : a History of the Yale Divinity School Library Collections" by Paul F. Stuehrenberg (1992; Yale Divinity School Library occasional publication ; no. 1, 1994 rev. ed.); The Day Missions Library centennial volume by Stephen L. Peterson, Paul F. Stuehrenberg, Martha Lund Smalley ; edited by Martha Lund Smalley. (Occasional publication / Yale Divinity School Library ; no. 2, 1993)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records document the development and growth of the Library of the Yale Divinity School, one of the foremost theological libraries in the world. The historical material dates from the 19th century, though the bulk is from the 20th century to present day. Included are the files and annual reports of Divinity Librarians Raymond Morris, Stephen L. Peterson and Paul F. Stuehrenberg. The international activities of the library are evident in grant funded projects that document, preserve and improve access to specific collections. Library departmental files from Technical Services and Special Collections show how the Yale Divinity Library has evolved over time through the implementation of technology to aid access, acquisitions, cataloging and description. Finally, publications and photographs, provide visual evidence of changes, developments and renovations of the library as it preserves the past and looks to the future.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series 1 Library History and Development Series 2 Librarian Files Series 3 Documentation and Preservation Projects Series 4 Funds and Donations Series 5 Technical Services and Technology Series 6 Special Collections Series 7 Publications and Exhibits Series 8 Photographs

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to Divinity Library staff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes visitor logs and reference statistics

scopecontent.p: Includes visitor logs and reference statistics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 1: A Library Worthy of the School: a History of the Yale Divinity School Library Collections, by Paul F. Stuehrenberg. (Revised edition 1994)
No. 2: The Day Missions Library Centennial Volume, by Stephen L. Peterson, Paul F. Stuehrenberg, Martha Lund Smalley (1993)
No. 3: The Legacy of John R. Mott, by Martha Lund Smalley (1993)
No. 4: Eli Smith and the Arabic Bible, by Margaret R. Leavy (1993)

No. 5: Communications from the Field: Missionary Postcards from Africa, edited by Martha Lund Smalley. Text from an exhibit at the Yale Divinity Library, July 1 - October 15, 1994 (1994, revised 2006)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 6: Women's Role in the History of the World Student Christian Federation, 1895-1945, by Johanna M. Selles. Commissioned to commemorate the centennial of the founding of the World Student Christian Federation, 1895-1945 (1995)
No. 7: Guide to Archives and Manuscript Collections at the Yale Divinity School Library, compiled by Martha Lund Smalley and Joan R. Duffy (1995, revised 1998)
No. 8: A History of the Expansion of Christianity Reconsidered: the Legacy of George E. Day, delivered by Andrew F. Walls on June 9, 1995 (1996)
No. 9: American Missionary Eyewitnesses to the Nanking Massacre, 1937-1938, edited by Martha Lund Smalley (1997)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 10: The American Revolution and the Roots of Mission in Africa: An Essay on Antislavery and Antistructure, by Lamin Sanneh (1997)
No. 11: Glimpses of Canton: the Diary of Elijah C. Bridgman, 1834-1838, edited by Martha Lund Smalley (1998)
No. 12: Robert Lowry Calhoun as Historian of Doctrine, delivered by George A. Lindbeck on May 29, 1998 (1998)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 13: Christian Mission and Islamic Studies: Beyond Antithesis, delivered by David A. Kerr in July 1999 (1999)
No. 14: Ts'ai Yung-ch'un's Life and Work: Fully Chinese and Fully Christian, by Hugh Barbour (2000)
No. 15: Bulldozers on the Quad: Yale Divinity School Library's Ten Year Report, by Paul F. Stuehrenberg (2001)
No. 16: Mission Dilemmas: Bride Price, Minor Marriage, Concubinage, Infanticide, and Education of Women, delivered by Jessie G. Lutz on July 13, 2001 (2002)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 17: Not the Bloom but the Root: Conversion and its Consequences in Nineteenth-Century Protestant Missionary Discourse, delivered by Jonathan J. Bonk on July 4, 2003 (2003)
No. 18: In Search of Moslems in China: the Story of a Journey through Northwest China, April 30-July 2, 1936, by Claude L. Pickens, Jr., edited by Martha Smalley (2004)
No. 19: St. Patrick and Bernard Mizeki: Missionary Saints and the Creation of Christian Communities, delivered by Dana L. Robert on July 8, 2005 (2005)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 20: Capturing the Image: African Missionary Photography as Enslavement and Liberation, delivered by T. Jack Thompson on June 29, 2007 (2007)
No. 21: From "the poor heathen" to "the glory and honour of all nations": Vocabularies of Race and Custom in Protestant Missions, 1844-1928, delivered by Brian Stanley on June 3, 2009 (2009)
No. 22: The Other Side of the Jordan: Yale Divinity Library Ten-Year Report, by Paul F. Stuehrenberg (2012)
No. 23: "For the Healing of the Nations": Religious Visions, Subterranean Universes, and Cross-Cultural Encounters, delivered by J. Nelson Jennings on June 28, 2013 (2013)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 24: European and Indian Discoveries, Definitions and Portrayals of Indic Religions: A Case Study of 18th Century, delivered by Daniel Jeyaraj on June 26, 2015 (2015)
No. 25: Exiles in their Own Land: Japanese Protestant Mission History and Theory in Conversation with Practical Theology, delivered by Thomas John Hastings on June 30, 2017 (2017)
No. 26: To be Agents of a Life-Giving Transformation: Christian Higher Education in Africa with Response, delivered by Joel A. Carpenter and Andrew F. Walls on June 28, 2019 (2019)

scopecontent.p: History of the Divinity Library; Deans of the Divinity School; Biblical Scholarship at YDS; Theology at YDS; Teaching
